corpus_building.implemented_as: The TXT paper is converted into a section-aware annotated mini-corpus.

tokenization.implemented_as: Every concept phrase and sentence is normalized into tokens for counts and n-grams.

normalization.implemented_as: Lower-casing and punctuation cleanup are used for phrase matching and deduplication.

n_gram.implemented_as: Concept length and document top unigrams/bigrams/trigrams are exposed.

bow_and_naive_bayes.implemented_as: Section BoW hits act as class-conditional section evidence for intro/method/experiment/conclusion style decisions.

tf_idf_document_term_matrix.implemented_as: Document-level frequency and TF-IDF matrices expose which section concepts are characteristic of this paper.

laplace_smoothing.implemented_as: P(section | concept) uses add-one smoothing, so unseen section-concept pairs are not forced to zero.

evidence_cue_lexicon.implemented_as: Cue phrases such as propose, achieve, outperform, and BLEU give transparent rule-based evidence hints.

sequence_labeling.implemented_as: The first model contributes BIO boundary evidence for concept phrase extraction.

canonicalization_noisy_channel_style.implemented_as: Observed surface phrases are mapped to canonical terms with an alias confidence score.

location.sentence: Alternatively, we can use standard first order gradient descent without preconditioning, but change the parameterization of our model to give gradients that are more like the whitened natural gradients of these methods.

context_window.previous: Such an approximate inverse can for example be obtained by using a Kronecker factored approximation to the Fisher matrix and inverting it (KFAC, [ 19 ]), by using an arXiv:1602.07868v3 [cs.LG] 4 Jun 2016 approximate Cholesky factorization of the inverse Fisher matrix (FANG, [ 8 ]), or by whitening the input of each layer in the neural network (PRONG, [3]).
context_window.current: Alternatively, we can use standard first order gradient descent without preconditioning, but change the parameterization of our model to give gradients that are more like the whitened natural gradients of these methods.
context_window.next: For example, Raiko et al. [ 23 ] propose to transform the outputs of each neuron to have zero output and zero slope on average.

location.sentence: Several authors have recently developed methods to improve the conditioning of the cost gradient for general neural network architectures.

context_window.previous: Improving the general optimizability of deep networks is a challenging task [ 4 ], but since many neural architectures share these basic building blocks, improving these building blocks improves the performance of a very wide range of model architectures and could thus be very useful.
context_window.current: Several authors have recently developed methods to improve the conditioning of the cost gradient for general neural network architectures.
context_window.next: One approach is to explicitly left multiply the cost gradient with an approximate inverse of the Fisher information matrix, thereby obtaining an approximately whitened natural gradient .

top_candidates.sentence: Next, we test the effect of weight normalization applied to deep convolutional variational autoencoders (CVAEs) , trained on the MNIST data set of images of handwritten digits and the CIFAR-10 data set of small natural images.
For CIFAR-10, we used a neural architecture with ResNet units and multiple intermediate stochastic layers 1 .
The decoder has a similar architecture, but with reversed direction.
We present weight normalization: a reparameterization of the weight vectors in a neural network that decouples the length of those weight vectors from their direction.
Our reparameterization is inspired by batch normalization but does not introduce any dependencies between the examples in a minibatch.
For example, Raiko et al. propose to transform the outputs of each neuron to have zero output and zero slope on average.

Following this second approach to approximate natural gradient optimization, we propose a simple but general method, called weight normalization, for improving the optimizability of the weights of neural network models.
As far as we are aware, the result with mean-only batch normalization combined with weight normalization represents the state-of-the-art for CIFAR-10 among methods that do not use data augmentation.

The reparameterization method is easy to apply, has low computational overhead, and does not introduce dependencies between the examples in a minibatch, making it our default choice in the development of new deep learning architectures.
